skill.name: azure-search-documents-ts
skill.description: Build search applications using Azure AI Search SDK for JavaScript (@azure/search-documents). Use when creating/managing indexes, implementing vector/hybrid search, semantic ranking, or building agentic retrieval with knowledge bases.
skill.summary: Build search applications using Azure AI Search SDK for JavaScript (@azure/search-documents). Use wh...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but context review found a Markdown guide with TypeScript examples for Azure AI Search. The command-execution, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and critical combination findings are false positives from code fences, template literals, SDK terms, and placeholder configuration. The remaining risk is low because the skill documents legitimate networked Azure SDK usage and environment-variable configuration.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detections
low_findings.description: The static command findings are Markdown code fences and TypeScript template literals in documentation. The only shell command shown is a normal npm install command for the Azure SDK, and no skill code executes user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review confirms these matches are documentation delimiters, npm installation text, or TypeScript string interpolation. There is no Ruby backtick execution or command-dispatching code in the skill.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Azure Endpoint and Environment Configuration
low_findings.description: The network and environment findings document Azure Search configuration. The endpoint is a placeholder service URL, and process.env reads only the endpoint and index name in the shown authentication example.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are normal configuration examples for an Azure SDK skill. No evidence shows secrets sent to a third-party endpoint.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings match ordinary Azure Search terms and document field names such as description. No hashing, encryption, signing, cipher selection, or weak cryptographic algorithm appears in the cited text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain search descriptions, selected fields, and suggester fields. None contain cryptographic API calls or algorithm configuration.

low_findings.title: False Positive Reconnaissance and Combination Heuristic
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance matches are the word search in the skill description and best practices. The critical dangerous-combination heuristic is dismissed because the command and credential components are false positives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The related evidence is visible in a short Markdown guide and does not include host inspection, privilege checks, or data collection. No prompt injection text was found during review.

content.user_title: Build Azure AI Search Apps in TypeScript
content.value_statement: Search apps need reliable Azure AI Search patterns for indexes, vectors, filters, and ranking. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through TypeScript SDK workflows.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install and import the Azure Search Documents SDK for TypeScript.
Explains authentication with Azure Identity and environment-based endpoint settings.
Provides patterns for creating indexes with vector fields and semantic configuration.
Demonstrates document upload, batch indexing, merge, and delete operations.
Covers full-text, vector, hybrid, semantic, autocomplete, suggestion, filter, and facet queries.
limitations: Does not generate embeddings; it expects an existing embedding function.
Does not provision Azure resources or create a search service.
Does not include production access-control implementation beyond filter guidance.
Does not replace official Azure SDK documentation for version-specific API changes.

use_cases.title: Prototype Product Search
use_cases.description: Create an Azure AI Search index, upload sample product documents, and test text, filter, and facet queries.

use_cases.title: Add Vector Retrieval
use_cases.description: Plan vector fields, upload embeddings, and combine nearest-neighbor search with keyword search in TypeScript.

use_cases.title: Improve Search Relevance
use_cases.description: Use semantic ranking, captions, answers, autocomplete, and suggestions to improve search result quality.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Search Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to help me set up a TypeScript Azure AI Search client with environment variables and Azure Identity.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Search Index
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an Azure AI Search index for my documents. Include searchable, filterable, facetable, and vector fields.

prompt_templates.title: Build Hybrid Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to implement hybrid search in TypeScript for my app. Combine keyword search, vector search, filters, and result selection.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Semantic Retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my Azure AI Search retrieval plan. Add semantic ranking, captions, answers, suggesters, and batch indexing guidance.

output_examples.input: I need product search with filters and categories.
output_examples.output: A TypeScript implementation plan with index fields, category filters, facet settings, document upload steps, and a basic search flow.

output_examples.input: I want vector search for support articles.
output_examples.output: A retrieval design covering embedding storage, vector dimensions, nearest-neighbor settings, selected result fields, and integration points for embedding generation.

output_examples.input: My search results are not relevant enough.
output_examples.output: A relevance checklist using hybrid search, semantic configuration, extractive captions, answer extraction, and query performance considerations.

best_practices: Use Azure Identity where possible and keep service keys outside source control.
Combine text search, vector search, filters, and semantic ranking for stronger relevance.
Batch document indexing and use incremental updates for production data changes.
anti_patterns: Do not paste Azure admin keys into prompts, logs, or committed files.
Do not use vector search alone when keyword filters or facets are required.
Do not request total counts on every production query without measuring latency impact.

faq.question: What SDK does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers the Azure AI Search Documents SDK for JavaScript and TypeScript, published as @azure/search-documents.

faq.question: Can it create an Azure Search service?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on application code, indexes, documents, and queries after the Azure service exists.

faq.question: Does it support vector search?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows vector fields, vector profiles, nearest-neighbor queries, and hybrid search patterns.

faq.question: Does it generate embeddings?
faq.answer: No. The examples expect an embedding function or service that you provide separately.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it for implementation?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides TypeScript patterns that Claude Code, Codex, and Claude can adapt to a project.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation-only examples with low risk and no confirmed malicious behavior.
